# Conroe ISD Board Meeting — 2017-09-19

---

# Unofficial Meeting Notes
## Conroe Independent School District Board of Trustees
### Regular Meeting — September 19, 2017

---

> **Note:** These are **unofficial notes** based on an auto-generated transcript of the board meeting. They may contain errors or omissions due to transcript quality.

---

## Opening

- Meeting called to order at 6:00 PM. Required members present; meeting posted in accordance with the Texas Open Meetings Act.
- **Color Guard Presentation:** Conroe High School Army JROTC presented the colors, led by Captain Williams.
- **Invocation:** Mr. Hubert delivered the invocation, which included prayers for those affected by recent flooding in Texas, Louisiana, Florida, Alabama, and Tennessee.
- **Pledges:** Mr. Williams led the U.S. and Texas pledges.

---

## Item 2A: Special District Recognition — Read for a Better Life Initiative

- **Presenter:** Dr. Stockton / Dr. Knowl
- This marks the **11th year** of the Read for a Better Life initiative in the district.
- Parents and community members are encouraged to spend **30 minutes a day** reading with children.
- Students from Mrs. Johnson's class at **Rice Elementary** (the "Rice Raccoons") were special guests; they introduced themselves individually.
- Dr. Stockton read the book *Duncan the Story Dragon* aloud to the students.
- The class presented a handmade card to Dr. Stockton.

---

## Item 2B: Special District Recognition — The Woodlands High School, Class 6A Lone Star Cup Winner

- **Presenters:** Principal Colcian and Athletic Director/Head Football Coach Mark Shmitt
- The Woodlands High School was recognized for winning the **2017 UIL Lone Star Cup** (Class 6A).
- This is the school's **5th or 6th Lone Star Cup championship in the last 10 years**.
- The campus has approximately **4,300 students**.
- Coaches and staff introduced individually included: Deena Grace (girls soccer/female athletic coordinator), Dale Reed (boys basketball), Trista Tatch (girls basketball), Erin Laskie (boys golf), Chad Hanly (girls golf), Jeremy Wade (swim/dive), Hans Kliedshmit (boys soccer), Tim Borch (softball), Jaris Green (boys track/cross country), Noel Hanson (girls track/cross country), and athletic secretary Patty Lursie.
- Board members praised the **culture of excellence** and the coaching staff's commitment to developing all athletes, not just top-tier performers.

---

## Item 3: Public Participation

### Registered Speakers:

1. **Christie Swaboda** (Educational Para-Professional, Bozman Intermediate; representing TSTA Conroe, 400+ members):
   - Thanked the board for:
     - Listening to concerns about the early start date
     - Para-professional pay increases, including the extra 1% for para-professionals
     - Consideration of paying employees for days missed due to Hurricane Harvey
   - Praised the district's leadership before, during, and after Hurricane Harvey, contrasting it favorably with another unnamed district.

2. **Steward Shrader** (Resident, Village of Concrides Crossing in the Willows):
   - Spoke in support of **agenda items 6A and 6B** (property reappraisal/tax relief for flood-damaged properties).
   - Noted 6 homes in his neighborhood were flooded by Hurricane Harvey; for some it was the **4th or 5th time since Tropical Storm Allison (2001)**.
   - Stated property tax relief is "absolutely essential" for homeowner recovery.

3. **Matt Dezely** (arrived after the 5:30 PM sign-up deadline; board allowed him to speak):
   - Expressed opposition to the district's **transportation policy** regarding the 1-mile walk zone for bus service.
   - Has started a **petition with 1,100 signatures**.
   - Described the difficulty of the walk (referencing HEB at 105/336 as approximately one mile; the courthouse downtown as 1.9 miles, including crossing I-45).
   - Referenced a conversation with **Trustee Williams**, who Mr. Dezely said expressed "no sympathy" for taxpayers within the mile zone.
   - Plans to present the petition at a **tentatively scheduled October transportation workshop**.
   - Board noted this was **not a posted agenda item** and could not comment or act.

---

## Item 3: Consent Agenda

- **Item G** was pulled from the consent agenda at the request of a board member.
- **Vote on remaining consent agenda items:** Motion to adopt consent agenda (sans Item G) — **Passed unanimously** (voice vote, all ayes).

### Item 3G (Voted Separately): Approval of 2017-2018 Estimated Annual Expenditures by Category
- Authorizes Dr. Stockton to negotiate and execute contracts with selected vendors, not to exceed aggregate estimates for each category.
- One board member (**Mr. Husbands**) requested removal; he **abstained** from the vote due to his company being listed as a vendor.
- **Vote: Passed** (with one abstention).

---

## Item 4A: Consider Approval of Read for a Better Life Resolution

- **Presenter:** Dr. Knowl
- Resolution proclaims that CISD staff will support Read for a Better Life and authorizes the district to enlist parent/community support for reading aloud to every student 30 minutes every day.
- **Vote: Passed unanimously.**

---

## Item 4B: Consider Approval of Targeted Improvement Plan for Sam Houston Elementary

- **Presenter:** Dr. Debby Phillips
- **Background:** Sam Houston Elementary received an **"Improvement Required"** rating. The school did not meet performance index targets on Indexes 1, 2, or 3 but **did meet** Index 4 (post-secondary readiness).
- **New Principal:** Vivianna Harris (previously at Anderson), praised for already making an impact.
- The district is participating in a **pilot program with Region 6** Education Service Center to provide enhanced support.
- Key personnel: Laura Reading (Region 6 school improvement lead), Shelly Winkler (district coordinator), Delise Loyd (school improvement professional, returning for continuity).
- **Plan Goals:**
  - **Goal 1 (Reading):** 40% of eligible students will exceed progress on the 2018 STAAR reading assessment (targeting 1.5 years of growth).
  - **Goal 2 (Writing):** 65% of eligible students will approach grade-level standards on the writing assessment.
  - **Goal 3 (Math):** 40% of eligible students will exceed progress on the STAAR math assessment.
- Focus is on building on prior year's work (guided reading, writers' workshop, small group math) by strengthening **professional learning communities** and team planning.
- Board members expressed strong support and praised the new leadership.
- **Vote: Passed unanimously.**

---

## Item 5A & 5B: Discuss and Name Flex School 18

### Discussion (5A):
Three names were proposed by board members:
1. **Catherine (Cathy) Clark** — nominated by a board member; had an active role in the Oak Ridge feeder zone
2. **Annette Gordon Reed** — nominated by Mr. Husbands; noted as a Pulitzer Prize winner from Conroe, TX
3. **Herbert "Kicks" Lamb** — nominated by a board member; a beloved former teacher

Board members acknowledged all three as worthy candidates and noted that **two additional schools** will need to be named in the Conroe area in the coming years.

### Vote (5B):
- It was noted that Catherine Clark had a direct connection to the **Oak Ridge feeder zone** where the school is located.
- **Motion:** Name the school **Catherine Johnson Clark Intermediate School**.
- **Vote: Passed unanimously.** 🎉
- Dr. Stockton attempted to call Catherine Clark to share the news (reached voicemail/answering machine).
- Community members with other name suggestions were encouraged to **resubmit names** when the next schools are named.

---

## Item 5C: Capital Improvement Update

- **Presenter:** Mr. Foster

| Project | Status |
|---|---|
| **Network Operation Center** | Wrapping up within weeks; old data center space being converted to usable office space |
| **Safety & Security (Phase 2)** | On schedule; wiring, cameras, door contacts being installed; Phase 3 planning ahead; completion target end of December |
| **Grand Oaks High School** | On schedule for **August 2018** opening; exterior being secured; academic wing nearing finishes; athletics/fine arts progressing |
| **Catherine Johnson Clark Intermediate (Flex 18)** | On schedule for **August 2018** opening; building structure and slab in progress; site development underway |
| **Conroe High School Addition/Renovation** | On schedule; building addition progressing; new central plant (HVAC) under construction; project runs through **December 2019** |

---

## Item 6A & 6B: Discuss Possible Reappraisal of Property Damaged / Consider Tax Relief

- **⚠️ ITEMS REMOVED FROM AGENDA**
- A board member moved to **remove Items 6A and 6B from the agenda**, arguing they were outside the board's scope of authority and more appropriately a state-level decision.
- Another board member objected, noting that the **state comptroller** had indicated reappraisal decisions were up to **local taxing entities**, and that representatives from the Montgomery County Central Appraisal District and Texas tax assessor-collector were present.
- A motion was made to **end discussion and move directly to vote**.
- **Vote to remove Items 6A and 6B from the agenda: Passed** (vote count not clearly stated in transcript; appeared to pass on voice vote with some opposition).
- **Notable:** This is a significant outcome given that a community member (Steward Shrader) had specifically spoken in favor of these items during public comment regarding Hurricane Harvey flood relief.

---

## Item 6C: Consider Award of RFP 17-01-01A (Contracted Educational Services, Professional Development, and Educational Consulting Services)

- Monthly recurring item; 17 vendors recommended for award.
- Estimated annual expenditure: approximately **$10,000**.
- 331 vendors solicited; 105 proposals received since June 2017.
- RFP remains open until May 31, 2018; contracts firm through September 30, 2018, with option to renew annually through September 30, 2022.
- **Vote: Passed unanimously.**

---

---

## Item 7A: Nominations for 2018-2019 Montgomery County Central Appraisal District Board of Directors

Three individuals were nominated:
1. **Barry D. Blanton** — Blanton Advisors LLC; finance degree; banking background; active in community (Leukemia & Lymphoma Society); Conroe High School graduate
2. **Bruce Tuft** — Current board member who expressed interest in continuing
3. **Bonar Luzay** — Bio to be provided via email

- **Vote on resolution to nominate all three: Passed.**
- **Board President Bush abstained** due to a business relationship with Mr. Blanton. She also disclosed he was her campaign treasurer in a prior uncontested race.

---

## Executive Session

- The board recessed for executive session (details of discussion not public).

---

## Post-Executive Session Action

### Hearing Officer Decision (Finegan matter):
- **Motion:** Uphold the decision of the hearing officer and deny any relief to Mr. Finegan.
- **Vote: Passed unanimously.**
- Hearing concluded.

---

## Adjournment

- Meeting proceeded toward adjournment following the executive session action.
